pi_items.source: Explicitly defined in the task description instructions for the agent to output.
Executing a PySAM Pywatts8 simulation with the provided Phoenix weather data yields an annual energy of ~105,036,513 kWh (Premium) and ~105,945,414 kWh (Thin Film), bounded correctly by this minimum.
Executing a PySAM Pywatts8 simulation with the provided Phoenix weather data yields an annual energy of ~105,036,513 kWh (Premium) and ~105,945,414 kWh (Thin Film), bounded correctly by this maximum.
Executing a PySAM simulation yields a summer (Jun-Aug) energy fraction of ~0.3075 (Premium) and ~0.3081 (Thin Film), bounded correctly by this minimum.
Executing a PySAM simulation yields a summer (Jun-Aug) energy fraction of ~0.3075 (Premium) and ~0.3081 (Thin Film), bounded correctly by this maximum.
Executing a PySAM simulation yields a winter (Dec-Feb) energy fraction of ~0.1678 (Premium) and ~0.1675 (Thin Film), bounded correctly by this minimum.
Executing a PySAM simulation yields a winter (Dec-Feb) energy fraction of ~0.1678 (Premium) and ~0.1675 (Thin Film), bounded correctly by this maximum.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on a real NREL National Solar Radiation Database (NSRDB) TMY weather file for Phoenix, AZ. Running a reference PySAM Pywatts8 simulation with the specified 50 MW DC configuration validates the verifier constraints: the calculated annual energy (~105 million kWh) falls exactly within the [100000000, 130000000] range, the summer energy fraction (~30.7%) falls within [0.28, 0.38], and the winter energy fraction (~16.7%) falls within [0.15, 0.25]. The expected output file 'temp_coef_comparison.json' correctly matches the task description requirements.
